mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in falling corporate revenues and rising recession probabilities.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.4pp threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during expansionary phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, driving broad-based corporate earnings growth that compounds into positive equity returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both grow > 1% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term shows zero marginal explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and fading economic momentum, prompting short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal reverses direction when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A declining inflation trajectory alongside stable policy rates signals successful disinflation without aggressive monetary tightening, preserving corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive real rate spreads consistently precede negative 20-day equity returns, or the interaction term shows zero marginal explanatory power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect structural labor market weakness that erodes consumer purchasing power and corporate profit margins, systematically depressing S&P 500 valuations over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show a positive or neutral correlation with PAYEMS declines over any 3-year window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms robust aggregate demand that outpaces baseline expectations, triggering immediate algorithmic buying and short covering that pushes S&P 500 prices higher within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in over 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses the 3.0% threshold, or the signal loses significance during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals restored supply chain efficiency and rising corporate output, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansions in recovery regimes fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of historical instances, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4%.
